summary: Round 2 re-review of Task 05 live-PG integration tests. All four round-1 major findings are resolved with meaningful, non-tautological, non-flaky assertions. Query tests now QueryRow().Scan into the exact receivers collectOverviewStat uses (arity/type drift now fails at the query layer). tps/others are asserted against an independently-recomputed exact formula with itv=2 making the /itv division observable. cache-hit ratio has a pure-function table test (incl. division-by-zero and negative-denom) with an unconditional !IsNaN assert, plus a deterministic synthetic-prev wiring test. Archiving-backlog degradation is asserted through collectOverviewStat (degraded replication rows do not blank count/size/cache). Both round-1 minors (verbose-gate compact-path untouched; bgwriter by-name columns) are addressed. The recovery-'t' substring-only check remains by design and is acceptable for a primary-only test fleet.

findings.issue: The deterministic division-by-zero wiring assertion is still guarded by `if noio.BlksHit == prevEqual.BlksHit && noio.BlksRead == prevEqual.BlksRead`. On a live cluster the re-read counters are not guaranteed to equal the prev (background blks I/O between ticks), so the inner asserts (CacheHitRatioValid==false, !IsNaN) can be skipped entirely, silently passing. The division-by-zero behavior is already covered unconditionally by the pure-function Test_cacheHitRatio (`no io -> n/a`), so the residual risk is low, but this collect-level branch is not deterministically exercised.
findings.recommendation: This is acceptable as-is because Test_cacheHitRatio covers denom==0 -> (0,false) and !IsNaN unconditionally. If tighter wiring coverage is wanted later, extract the prev/curr delta computation so the n/a path can be asserted on a synthetic curr without depending on live counter stability, or assert that when the guard is false the ratio is still a valid percentage in [0,100] (an else branch with a real assertion) so the test never no-ops.

findings.issue: recovery-'t' (standby) WAL-function substitution is verified by substring assertion only (Contains pg_last_wal_receive_lsn); the standby form is never executed against PG. Noted by the author as intentional: the test fleet are primaries and executing standby WAL functions errors there.
findings.recommendation: Intentional and acceptable given a primary-only fixture fleet. The substring check still protects against template wiring regressions (wrong function name, unsubstituted {{). No change required unless a standby fixture becomes available.
